name: Deepnote Collaborative Data Notebook API
what_it_does: Collaborative data science notebook platform with REST API — providing Jupyter-compatible notebooks with real-time collaboration, scheduled execution, version control, and data integrations, optimized for data teams working together.
use_cases: Triggering notebook execution programmatically for scheduled data pipeline runs
Retrieving notebook outputs and results for downstream pipeline consumption
Managing workspace users and projects via API for team administration
Building data workflows that execute Deepnote notebooks as computation steps
Automating notebook deployment and scheduling for recurring analytics
not_for: High-performance compute requiring dedicated GPU clusters (use JupyterHub or Modal)
Production ML model serving (notebooks are for exploration, not serving)
Teams needing self-hosted Jupyter environment without cloud dependency
best_when: A data team needs collaborative notebooks with scheduled execution, real-time collaboration, and API-triggered runs as a hosted Jupyter alternative.
avoid_when: You need self-hosted notebooks, production ML serving, or dedicated GPU compute management.

auth.notes: API key from Deepnote account settings. Bearer token authentication. Workspace-scoped access. API access on paid plans only — free tier has limited API access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 750 compute hours/month, limited projects, no API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Team from $12/user/month; Business custom; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: API access requires paid plan. Collaborative features make it strong for data teams. Jupyter-compatible — existing notebooks work. Czech company. Popular in data science community.

performance.rate_limits: Execution rate limits per plan; API calls limited per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for notebook execution and workspace management. Notebook execution is async — must poll for completion. API access on paid plans only. Documentation covers core execution workflow.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Notebook execution triggers create new runs. Status polling is idempotent. Result retrieval is idempotent after completion. Workspace management operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires paid plan — free tier testing not possible with API
Notebook execution is async — agents must implement polling for completion
Execution timeout applies — long-running notebooks may need chunking
Notebook environment setup (dependencies) must be configured in UI before API execution

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. Notebook code and data in Deepnote cloud — verify data security for sensitive data processing.
